title: Multi-Agent Conductor Orchestration Workflow
summary: OpenCode-native six-stage Conductor pipeline. Design approved 2026-06-28 and implemented the same day: command + orchestrator + 6 model-pinned stage subagents + skill reference pack. Statically verified (agents load, models resolve). Runtime dry-run/gate/failure-sim checks pending an OpenCode restart.

approval.openFollowup: gpt-5.5-fast emergency fallback deferred to future tuning

keyOutcomes: Mapped standard Espanso snippets to all six pipeline stages
Recommended command + orchestrator agent + stage subagent architecture
Defined threshold options, handoff artifacts, and fallback paths
Secured Dave approval of architecture, autonomy, and threshold defaults (2026-06-28)
Implemented and statically verified all command/agent/skill files (2026-06-28)
risks: Final agent syntax must express GPT-5.5 Low as model openai/gpt-5.5 plus variant low (mitigated: variant field is a first-class frontmatter field)
OpenCode commands do not provide native declarative branching, so the orchestrator prompt enforces conditions
Write-enabled subagents are narrowly permissioned (executor edit/bash allow; others .conductor-only or read-only)
Full-auto autonomy removes the pre-execution human checkpoint; safety relies on the execution-failure stop rule and iteration caps
Files placed in agent\ and skill\ (singular), not agents\ and skills\ (plural) as the plan text wrote, to match the live scanned config
dependencies: Live smoke test that the runtime accepts the model pins (statically MET via opencode models; runtime pending dry-run)
blocking: OpenCode restart required, then live dry-run + runtime gate/diversity/failure-sim verification (Phase 3 item 7, Phase 4 items 3-7)
deliverables: plan.md (Phase 0-3 done; Phase 3.7 + Phase 4.3-7 runtime verification pending restart)
command + orchestrator + 6 subagents + skill pack + README (implemented)
validationCriteria: All six stages map to exact standard prompts
Diversity rules are specified and enforceable
Fallback and rollback paths are documented
